body.title: Document turn_pipeline.py completeness as palace subsystem entry
body.result: Added theigors/subsystem_index/turn_pipeline node — status BUILT-BUT-GATED-OFF, references T-retire-legacy-direct-reasoner-path as cutover ticket.
body.description:
cognition/turn_pipeline.py is a 5-stage conductor (cascade → workflow → decision_blob → voice_context → voice production) but is gated off (IGOR_TURN_PIPELINE=false). Status was unclear from outside: built? half-built? abandoned? — making T-retire-legacy-direct-reasoner-path harder to plan.

DONE 2026-04-27: Added palace node theigors/subsystem_index/turn_pipeline. Records: status=BUILT-BUT-GATED-OFF, primary file with docstring, 5-stage pipeline shape, CP grounding, originating tickets, cutover ticket reference (T-retire-legacy-direct-reasoner-path).

Affected files:
- clan.memory_palace path='theigors/subsystem_index/turn_pipeline' (INSERT).

Design rules:
- docs-in-code: palace node points at top-of-file docstring of turn_pipeline.py (already canonical — confirmed thorough docstring exists).
- postgres-only: ✓.
- no-new-memory-schemas: ✓.
- test-plan-or-why-not: verification IS reading the SELECT.

Scope boundary:
IN: subsystem_index node for turn_pipeline.
OUT: cutting over from legacy (that's T-retire-legacy-direct-reasoner-path, L), modifying turn_pipeline.py, flipping IGOR_TURN_PIPELINE.

Test plan:
- SELECT from palace returns the new node.
- Verified 2026-04-27.

Follow-up: T-retire-legacy-direct-reasoner-path now has a clean status anchor.
